perturbation.original_text: The protec tions and restrictions in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of Proprietary Information shall not apply to any information which the Receiving Party can show by written and dated or datable material: (a) is, at the time of disclosure hereunder, already published or otherwise publicly available; or (b) is, after disclosure hereunder, published or becomes available to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; or (c) is rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession with rights to us e and/or disclose, prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party with rights to use and/or disclose; or (e) is independently developed by or for the Receiving Party without referenc e or access to Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; or (f) is the subject of a requirement of a court proceeding requirement on the Receiving Party for disclosure, in which event the Receiving Party shall give prompt notice thereof to the Disclosi ng Party to the extent permissible by law..
perturbation.changed_text: The protections and restrictions in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of Proprietary Information shall not apply to any information which the Receiving Party believes in good faith to be: (a) is, at the time of disclosure hereunder, already published or otherwise publicly available; or (b) is, after disclosure hereunder, published or becomes available to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; or (c) is rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession with rights to use and/or disclose, prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party with rights to use and/or disclose; or (e) is independently developed by or for the Receiving Party without referenc e or access to Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; or (f) is the subject of a requirement of a court proceeding requirement on the Receiving Party for disclosure, in which event the Receiving Party shall give prompt notice thereof to the Disclosi ng Party to the extent permissible by law..
perturbation.explanation: This modification weakens the exceptions to confidentiality by replacing the objective standard ('can show by written and dated or datable material') with a subjective belief ('believes in good faith to be'). This creates an ambiguity about the standard of proof required, potentially leading to disputes over what constitutes a valid exception. The in-text contradiction emerges when compared against Clause 2, which obligates strict confidentiality and provides no leeway for subjective interpretations. Now there is an exception to an exception which makes the initial protections of the NDA less effective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'written and dated or datable material' to prove an exception to confidentiality. The changed text introduces a subjective 'good faith' belief standard. This directly contradicts the strict confidentiality obligations in the Contradicted Text (Clause 4), which requires keeping Proprietary Information confidential and not disclosing it without written permission. The changed text weakens this obligation by allowing disclosure based on subjective belief, conflicting with the requirement for strict confidentiality. This fits the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where key obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information may survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may' in the survival clause, the obligation to maintain confidentiality after the agreement's termination becomes optional, which goes against the intent of protecting Proprietary Information long-term. This change introduces ambiguity. This contradicts Clause 2 which has no optional verbiage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'shall,' creating a mandatory obligation for confidentiality post-termination. The changed text uses 'may,' making the obligation optional. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party 'undertakes' to keep information confidential, implying a firm commitment, not an option. The change introduces ambiguity where certainty existed, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition provided for 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement shall not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorised representatives of all Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents a general understanding between the Parties concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement may be amended by informal communication between authorized representatives of all Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the initial intent of the NDA as a binding agreement and introduces ambiguity. The original clause established that the agreement was the entire and final understanding, unchangeable without formal written consent. By weakening that language, the binding nature of the contract comes into question, creating a contradiction. Now agreements can be made through informal communication, which is against the initial definition of how changes can be made within the agreement, weakening it's initial protections and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement the term "Proprietary Information" shall mean any information, whether in writing or other documentary form, in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of algorithms, samples or models, disclosed by one Party (the "Disclosing Party" ) to another Party (the "Receiving Party") under this Agreement, provided that: (a) where such information is in writing or other documentary form, it must be clearly and conspicuously marked at the time of disclosure as proprietary or commercially sensitive (for example with a marking such as "Proprietary" or "Commercial -in-Confidence").; or V1.2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE (WHEN COMPLETE) 2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENC E (WHEN COMPLETE) (b) where such information is disclosed in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of samples or models, it is designated proprietary or commercially sensitive at the time of disclosure and is confirmed by the Disclosing Party as such in documentary form within ninety (90) days from its being disclosed, in which event all the protections and restrictions in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of said Proprietary information shall apply retrospectively during the said period of ninety days. Proprietary Information shall not include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis of any hardware, software or material samples provided by the Disclosing Party, notwithstanding that the requirements for marking and designation referred to above shall not have been fulfilled.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement could only be amended by written agreement signed by authorized representatives. The changed text now allows for amendments through informal communication. This directly contradicts the nature of 'Proprietary Information' defined in the contradicted text. The contradicted text describes specific requirements for marking and designating information as proprietary to ensure its protection under the agreement. If the agreement can be informally amended, it undermines the clarity and enforceability of these protections, creating a contradiction between the amendment process and the reliance on clearly defined 'Proprietary Information'.
